title: Reading: The Detective's Interrogation
learningGoal: Read and analyze a police interrogation to identify who performed which actions, paying close attention to the 'ne' particle and verb gender agreements.
instructions: Inspector Sharma is interrogating two suspects, Rahul and Neha, about a stolen file. Notice how they use 'Maine' and 'Usne' to blame each other. Pay attention to the verb endings to see how they match the objects.
readingText:
इंस्पेक्टर: कल रात तुम दोनों कहाँ गए थे?
राहुल: सर, मैं कल रात घर गया। मैं बाहर नहीं गया।
नेहा: झूठ! यह बाहर गया। मैंने इसे देखा।
इंस्पेक्टर: नेहा, तुमने कल क्या किया?
नेहा: मैंने कल एक किताब पढ़ी। मैंने कुछ नहीं किया।
राहुल: सर, इसने मेरी किताब ली! और उसने फाइल भी देखी।
इंस्पेक्टर: फाइल किसने ली?
नेहा: मैंने फाइल नहीं ली। राहुल ने फाइल ली।
राहुल: नहीं सर! मैं तो सो गया। मुझे कुछ नहीं पता।
textInTargetLanguage:
इंस्पेक्टर: कल रात तुम दोनों कहाँ गए थे?
राहुल: सर, मैं कल रात घर गया। मैं बाहर नहीं गया।
नेहा: झूठ! यह बाहर गया। मैंने इसे देखा।
इंस्पेक्टर: नेहा, तुमने कल क्या किया?
नेहा: मैंने कल एक किताब पढ़ी। मैंने कुछ नहीं किया।
राहुल: सर, इसने मेरी किताब ली! और उसने फाइल भी देखी।
इंस्पेक्टर: फाइल किसने ली?
नेहा: मैंने फाइल नहीं ली। राहुल ने फाइल ली।
राहुल: नहीं सर! मैं तो सो गया। मुझे कुछ नहीं पता।
baseLanguageSupportText:
Inspector: Last night where did you both go?
Rahul: Sir, last night I went home. I did not go outside.
Neha: Lie! He went outside. I saw him.
Inspector: Neha, what did you do yesterday?
Neha: Yesterday I read a book. I did nothing.
Rahul: Sir, she took my book! And she also saw the file.
Inspector: Who took the file?
Neha: I did not take the file. Rahul took the file.
Rahul: No Sir! I slept. I know nothing.

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: Why does Rahul say 'Main ghar gaya' (मैं घर गया) without using the 'ne' particle?
options: Because 'Jaana' (to go) is an intransitive verb. Intransitive verbs do not take 'ne'.
Because Rahul forgot the grammar rules.

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: When Neha says she read a book, she says 'Maine kitaab PADHI' (मैंने किताब पढ़ी). Why is the verb 'padhi' (feminine) when 'Maine' is gender neutral?
options: Because 'kitaab' (book) is a feminine object, and the 'ne' particle forces the verb to match the object.
Because the verb padhna is always feminine.

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: Who claims they saw Rahul go outside?
What two things does Rahul accuse Neha of doing?
options: Taking his book (kitaab lee) and seeing the file (file dekhi).

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: Look at the Inspector's question: 'File kisne lee?' (फाइल किसने ली?). What does 'Kisne' mean?
options: Who (When used with a transitive verb in the past tense)
Rahul says he slept (so gaya) and knows nothing.

correctAnswers.rq_1: Because 'Jaana' (to go) is an intransitive verb. Intransitive verbs do not take 'ne'.
correctAnswers.rq_2: Because 'kitaab' (book) is a feminine object, and the 'ne' particle forces the verb to match the object.
correctAnswers.rq_4: Taking his book (kitaab lee) and seeing the file (file dekhi).
correctAnswers.rq_5: Who (When used with a transitive verb in the past tense)
correctAnswers.rq_6: Rahul says he slept (so gaya) and knows nothing.

expectedSummaryPoints: Distinguish intransitive verbs (gaya, so gaya) from transitive verbs with 'ne' (maine dekha, kisne lee).
Recognize that 'ne' blocks the subject gender, forcing the verb to match the object (kitaab padhi, file dekhi).

feedbackRules.onPass: Outstanding! You successfully decoded the complex grammar of a Hindi police interrogation.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the dialogue carefully. The word 'Maine' is 'I'. The word 'Isne' is 'He/She'. See how the verbs change based on what they touch.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Pay close attention to question 2. Neha says 'padhI' not because she is a woman, but because 'kitaab' is feminine!

audioAssets.audioTranscript: इंस्पेक्टर: कल रात तुम दोनों कहाँ गए थे? राहुल: सर, मैं कल रात घर गया। मैं बाहर नहीं गया। नेहा: झूठ! यह बाहर गया। मैंने इसे देखा। इंस्पेक्टर: नेहा, तुमने कल क्या किया? नेहा: मैंने कल एक किताब पढ़ी। मैंने कुछ नहीं किया। राहुल: सर, इसने मेरी किताब ली! और उसने फाइल भी देखी। इंस्पेक्टर: फाइल किसने ली? नेहा: मैंने फाइल नहीं ली। राहुल ने फाइल ली। राहुल: नहीं सर! मैं तो सो गया। मुझे कुछ नहीं पता।

hintText: Focus on the objects. 'Kitaab' (book) and 'File' are both feminine, so the verbs acting on them end in 'ee' (Padhi, Lee, Dekhi).
commonMistakes: Thinking 'Padhi' is feminine because Neha is speaking.
